steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the four angle measures
steps.content:
Two intersecting lines create four angles. Vertical angles are congruent; adjacent angles are supplementary (sum to $180^{\circ}$).

If one angle is $(7x - 260)^{\circ}$, its vertical partner is also $(7x - 260)^{\circ}$, and each of the two adjacent angles is $180^{\circ} - (7x - 260)^{\circ} = (440 - 7x)^{\circ}$.

So the four angles are two copies of $(7x-260)^{\circ}$ and two copies of $(440-7x)^{\circ}$, and each angle measure must be positive.

steps.title: List every possible sum of two angles
steps.content:
Choosing two of the four angles, the possible sums are:
• two acute (or two obtuse) of the same measure: ${2}(7x-260) = 14x - 520$, or ${2}(440-7x) = -14x + 880$.
• one of each measure (an adjacent pair): $(7x-260) + (440-7x) = 180$.

So the only attainable sums are $(14x-520)^{\circ}$, $(-14x+880)^{\circ}$, and $180^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Compare each choice to the attainable sums
steps.content:
• A. $(-14x+520)^{\circ}$: not equal to any of the three sums above. Also, since $(7x-260) \gt 0$ forces $x \gt 260/7 \approx 37.1$, we get $-14x+520 \lt 0$, which can't be a sum of two positive angles. Impossible.
  • B. $(-14x+880)^{\circ}$: equals ${2}(440-7x)$, the sum of the two obtuse angles. Possible.
  • C. $(14x-520)^{\circ}$: equals ${2}(7x-260)$, the sum of the two acute angles. Possible.
  • D. $180^{\circ}$: the sum of any adjacent (linear) pair. Possible.
That confirms A is correct.
